description: Simple variable substitution renders context value.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('Hello {{ name }}!')
print(t.render(name='Alice'))
print(t.render(name='Bob'))

description: Dot notation accesses dict keys and object attributes.
Index access and mixed dot/index notation.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ items[0] }} {{ items[1] }} {{ user.name }}')
print(t.render(items=['a', 'b'], user={'name': 'Alice'}))

description: If block renders content when condition is truthy.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if user %}Hello {{ user }}{% endif %}')
print(t.render(user='Alice'))
print(t.render(user=None))

description: If/else renders else branch when condition is falsy.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if logged_in %}Welcome!{% else %}Login{% endif %}')
print(t.render(logged_in=True))
print(t.render(logged_in=False))

description: Elif chain selects the first truthy branch.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if a %}A{% elif b %}B{% elif c %}C{% else %}D{% endif %}')
print(t.render(a=0, b=1, c=2))
print(t.render(a=0, b=0, c=0))

description: For loop iterates over a list and renders each item.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('<{% for x in xs %}{{ x }}{% endfor %}>')
print(t.render(xs=[1, 2, 3]))
print(t.render(xs=[]))

description: For loop provides loop.index (1-based) and loop.index0 (0-based).
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for item in items %}{{ loop.index }}:{{ item }} {% endfor %}')
print(t.render(items=['a', 'b', 'c']))

description: upper filter converts value to uppercase.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ name | upper }}')
print(t.render(name='alice'))

description: lower filter; filters can be chained with |.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ name | upper | lower }}')
print(t.render(name='Hello'))

description: default filter provides fallback for undefined, None, and empty string.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('[{{ a | default("x") }}][{{ b | default("y") }}][{{ c | default("z") }}]')
print(t.render(a='', b=None))

description: length filter returns len of value; trim strips whitespace.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ items | length }} / {{ msg | trim }}')
print(t.render(items=[1, 2, 3, 4], msg='  hello  '))

description: Include renders a sub-template from the environment loader.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(loader={'nav': 'NAV:{{ section }}'})
t = env.from_string('{% include "nav" %}|Body')
print(t.render(section='home'))

description: Comments are stripped from output entirely.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('A{# comment #}B {# another #}C')
print(t.render())

description: Undefined variable in {{ }} raises UndefinedError (subclass of NameError).
Unclosed if block raises TemplateSyntaxError (subclass of ValueError) at parse time.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template, TemplateSyntaxError
try:
    t = Template('{% if x %}no end')
    print('parsed')
except TemplateSyntaxError:
    print('syntax-error')
print(issubclass(TemplateSyntaxError, ValueError))

description: Missing include raises IncludeError (subclass of LookupError).
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment, IncludeError
env = Environment(loader={})
try:
    t = env.from_string('{% include "missing" %}')
    t.render()
    print('rendered')
except IncludeError:
    print('include-error')
print(issubclass(IncludeError, LookupError))

description: Custom filter registered via env.add_filter overrides built-in and is callable.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment()
env.add_filter('shout', lambda s: str(s).upper() + '!')
env.add_filter('double', lambda s: str(s) * 2)
t = env.from_string('{{ name | shout }} {{ name | double }}')
print(t.render(name='hi'))

description: Loop values pass through filters, then conditionals gate their output.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for n in nums %}{% if n %}{{ n | upper }}{% endif %}{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(nums=['a', '', 'b', None, 'c']))

description: Included template receives parent context, its output flows through parent's filter pipeline.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(loader={'greet': 'Hello {{ name }}'})
t = env.from_string('{% include "greet" %}{{ name | upper }}')
print(t.render(name='alice'))

description: Loop variable shadows outer context; nested if/for blocks accumulate correctly.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if show %}{% for item in items %}{{ prefix }}{{ item }}{% endfor %}{% endif %}')
print(t.render(show=True, items=['a', 'b'], prefix='<'))
print(t.render(show=False, items=['a', 'b'], prefix='<'))

description: Chained includes (A includes B includes C) accumulate output with shared context.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(loader={
    'a': 'A:{{ x }}{% include "b" %}',
    'b': 'B:{{ x }}{% include "c" %}',
    'c': 'C:{{ x }}'
})
t = env.from_string('{% include "a" %}')
print(t.render(x='v'))

description: Loop variable shadows same-named outer context variable; outer is restored after loop.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ x }}{% for x in items %}[{{ x }}]{% endfor %}{{ x }}')
print(t.render(x='outer', items=['a', 'b']))

description: Re-rendering the same Template with different contexts produces independent, correct output.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if a %}{{ a }}{% endif %}{% for x in xs %}{{ x }}{% endfor %}')
r1 = t.render(a='hi', xs=[1, 2])
r2 = t.render(a='', xs=[])
r3 = t.render(a='bye', xs=[9])
print(r1)
print(r2)
print(r3)

description: Failed include does not corrupt Environment; subsequent templates render correctly.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment, Template, IncludeError
env = Environment(loader={'ok': 'OK:{{ x }}'})
# First: a successful render
t1 = env.from_string('{% include "ok" %}')
print(t1.render(x='first'))
# Second: a failed include
try:
    t2 = env.from_string('{% include "bad" %}')
    t2.render()
except IncludeError:
    print('caught')
# Third: environment should still work
t3 = env.from_string('{% include "ok" %}')
print(t3.render(x='third'))

description: Undefined variable error in a specific render does not corrupt the Template for a later valid render.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template, UndefinedError
t = Template('{% for item in items %}{{ item }}{{ end }}{% endfor %}')
# Render with missing 'end' variable (should fail)
try:
    t.render(items=['a', 'b'])
    print('unexpected-success')
except UndefinedError:
    print('undefined-caught')
# Render with all variables present
print(t.render(items=['x', 'y'], end='.'))

description: Filter chain order matters: upper|lower vs lower|upper on mixed-case input produce different results.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t1 = Template('[{{ s | upper | lower }}]')
t2 = Template('[{{ s | lower | upper }}]')
print(t1.render(s='Hello'))
print(t2.render(s='Hello'))

description: Variables set in context are available before and after include; include order affects output position.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(loader={'part': '({{ v }})'})
t = env.from_string('A{% include "part" %}B{% include "part" %}C')
print(t.render(v='x'))

description: For loop iterates, includes sub-template, filters the included output, and conditionally renders.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Environment
env = Environment(loader={'item': '<{{ name | upper }}>'})
# Note: include inside the for loop; filter applied to loop var before passing
env.add_filter('wrap', lambda s: '[' + str(s) + ']')
t = env.from_string('{% for u in users %}{% if u.active %}{% include "item" %}{{ u.name | wrap }}{% endif %}{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(users=[
    {'name': 'alice', 'active': True},
    {'name': 'bob', 'active': False},
    {'name': 'carol', 'active': True}
]))

description: Comments, loops, filters, and conditionals compose cleanly: comments are stripped everywhere.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for x in xs %}{# loop-start #}{% if x %}{{ x | upper }}{% endif %}{# loop-end #}{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(xs=['a', '', 'b']))
